def: s.m. Anything of the shape of a flower; a speck in the eye, albugo; cataract; — anything swolien; roasted or parched grain
more: n. H. phūlnā to swell. 1. Any grain which swells by being roasted. 2. Any thing swollen, or in the shape of a flower. 3. A cataract (in the eye); albugo . Apnā ṭeṭṛā dekheṅ nahiṅ, dūsre kī phullī nihāreṅ . Prov. He cannot see the beam in his own eye, but he sees the mote in others. phullā nikālnā , v. a. To remove a cataract. Hai anjan taīyār, milā-ke ek bār jo ḍālūṅ, Ghar pe baiṭhe isī davā se phullā turat nikālūṅ . The medicine man's street cry. Chaubolā. An eye salve I have ready here, but once applied, I vouch, While sitting at your ease the cataract I'll instant couch.
s. (dakh.) Cataract (disease of the eye, Harris ): see پهلي phullī. پهلا نکالنا phullā nikālnā, To couch (an eye: Harris ).
subs: v.n. To remove a cataract, to couch.

ety: phūl˚ = Prk. फुल्ल(इ) = S. स्फुट(ति), rt. स्फुट; also फुल्ल(ति), rt. फुल्ल्, adopted fr. the Prk.
def: v.n. To blossom, blow, flower; to bloom, flourish, to be in health and spirits; — to swell, be inflated, be puffed out; to swell out, to expand (with joy), to be pleased; to be puffed up (with pride, &c.); to be big with child
more: v. n. See itrānā . 1. To swell; to be inflated, puffed up. Phūle phūle phirat haiṅ, 'āj hamāro biyāh,' Tulsī, gāë bajāë-ke diye kāṭh meṅ pāë . Dohā. They dance and swell, 'this is our wedding day,' enjoyment sweet, O Tulsī, in the stocks to dance and song they place their feet. 2. To be big with child. 3. To blossom; blow; flower ( khilnā ). 4. To be pleased; to be in health and spirits. phūlā na samānā , v. n. Not to be able to contain one's self through joy; to be overjoyed; to exult.
v. n. 1. To blossom, to blow, to flower. 2. To be pleased, to be in health and spirits, i. e. to bloom, to flourish. 3. To swell, to be inflated, to be puffed up (with pride, &c.).
subs: v.n. To expand with delight, be pleased or glad

ety: phūṅk˚ = Prk. फुक्क(इ) or फुक्के(इ), fr. S. फूत्+कृ;+nā = anā = Prk. अणअं = S. अनीयं
def: v.t. To blow, blow on (with the breath); to puff; to blow (a wind instrument); to blow up (fire, &c.); to set on fire, inflame, kindle; to blow or breathe a charm or incantation
more: v. a. 1. To blow with the breath. Dūdh kā jalā chhāch phūṅk phūṅk pītā hai . Prov. Who've been once scalded by hot milk blow ere they drink even buttermilk. (A burnt child dreads the fire). 2. To blow (a horn, trumpet, etc). Are papīhā, bāore, ādhī rain na kūk, Haure haure sulagtī, taiṅ kyoṅ dīnī phūṅk? Dohā. O cuckoo mad, why in the midnight wilt thou cry? I smould'ring slowly burn, why blazing match apply? 3. To blow a charm or spell; to exorcise. Jāne kyā phūṅk mār dī hai, dīvānah ho gayā . 4. To lavish; waste; throw away. 1. Ham ne saiṅkṛoṅ rupaë phūṅk dīye . 2. Ghar phūṅk tamāshā! Prov. Your house must burn if pleasure you would learn. (You must spend freely if you would know what enjoyment is). 5. To set fire to; blow up. 2. Ranthī sāji chale bandu bhāī, Phūṅk diye koī, koī soṅp diye dharnī . Kabir. Your bier laid out, your brethren bear you on your way, Some fire apply, some in th…
v. a. To blow with the breath; to blow up (a fire, &c.), inflame, set on fire, kindle, to blow (a horn, trumpet, &c.).
subs: v.t. (intensive), To blow away; to waste, squander, run through (wealth, &c.); to blow up (fire), to set fire to, to kindle, inflame; to grieve, afflict, distress, torment; — to puff up, to flatter; to breathe (into one's ear), to influence (one against another); to spread abroad (a report, &c.)
v.n. To tread or walk carefully; to act or proceed carefully or cautiously.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀨𑀼𑀓𑁆𑀓𑀤𑀺 (phukkadi), from Sanskrit *फूत्कति (*phūtkati), extended from the root फूत् (phūt) or perhaps a back-formation from फूत्कार (phūtkāra, “blowing”). Cognate with Nepali फुक्नु (phuknu), Gujarati ફૂંકવું (phū̃kvũ), Marathi फुंकणे (phuṅkṇe).

more: (dimin. of پهوڙا) s. f. A sore, a pimple, a bile.

def: v.n. To quiver, tremble, shiver; to flutter; to flap, to wave (as a flag, &c.); see phaṛphaṛānā.
more: v. n. H. phaṭ phaṭ . 1. To flap the wings; flutter. Kān phaṛ phaṛā ḍālo . 2. To move with convulsive motion; to struggle; to be agitated. Pāī khabar jo āmad-i-fasl-i-bahār kī: Kyā phaṛ-phaṛā-ke murg-i-giraftār rah gayā? Rind. Spring's harbinger a joyful message brings, Imprisoned birds are fluttering their wings! 3. To long for; to be anxious; to pine for.
v. n. 1. To tremble. 2. To wave (as, hair in the wind).

def: s.f. Trembling, quivering; tremour, palpitation; flutter
more: n. f. S. स्फुर shake. A flutter; trembling; shivering. phurphurī lenā , v. n. 1. To shake; tremble; shiver. 2. To wave (as hair in the wind).
s. f. Trembling, quivering, tremour, palpitation.

def: s.m. A buzzing or whizzing sound; whir (of a bird, &c.), whiz; the sound produced by the explosion of a little gunpowder.
more: s. m. The noise of a bird, as a partridge or quail, suddenly taking wing; or of a small quantity of gunpowder exploding.

def: s.f. = phurtī, q.v. s.f. Activity, quickness, alertness, briskness, nimbleness, speed; alacrity, promptness, readiness
subs: v.n. To be quick, active, or nimble; to make haste.

def: s.f. Activity, quickness, alertness, briskness, nimbleness, speed; alacrity, promptness, readiness
more: n. f. S. स्फूर्ति. 1. Quickness; alertness; alacrity; speed; briskness; promptness; promptitude; posthaste. Bandar kī turat, phurat, surat mashhūr hai . Prov. The monkey's cunning and agility are notorious. 2. Readiness; nimbleness; expedition. phurtī se, turat phurat , adv. Quickly; instantly. Phr. In double quick time; on eagle's wing. phurtī karnā , v. n. To make haste; look sharp; to be quick, active, stirring, on the alert; to cut along; dash along; keep moving; push on; go ahead. Phr. To stir one's stumps; to mend one's pace; to put one's best beg foremost.
(from پهرنا) part. f. or s. f. Returning, return, homeward-bound. پهرتي کابهاڙا phirtī kā bhāṛā, The hire of a return boat, carriage, &c.
subs: v.n. To be quick, active, or nimble; to make haste.

def: adj. Possessing activity, &c.; active, brisk, quick, smart, nimble, alert, expert, ready, prompt.
more: adj. Quick; nimble; active; smart; alert; expert; ready. Phr. Quick as a lamplighter; nimble as a squirrel.
adj. Quick, nimble, active, smart, alert, expert.

def: v.t. To cause to be broken, to get (a thing) broken (by).
s.m. Old dry grass or straw (esp. such as is used in thatching); rubbish, litter; anything weak, frail, or decayed (hence often conveniently rendered adjectively by 'weak,' 'frail,' &c.)
more: s. m. Old dry grass or straw. پهوس مين چنگاري ڐالنا phūs meṅ chingārī ḍālnā, To excite contention or strife.
subs: s.m. lit. 'Warming oneself before a fire of straw'; a vain or useless attempt; a vain hope
v.n. lit. To throw a spark into straw'; to excite contention or strife.

def: s.m. (Prov.) = pūs, q.v. s.m. The ninth solar month of the Hindūs (Dec.-Jan., the full moon of which is near pushya, three stars in Cancer).

ety: S. स्फुत्, or श्वस्, or onomat.
def: s.f. A soft sound (as of weak, flimsy, friable, or brittle substances); sound of soft ventris crepitatio; hissing, hiss
more: n. A hissing sound; a whisper.
subs: phas-phas, s.f. Hissing; whispering; — softness, looseness, flabbiness, flaccidity
s.m. Whispering, buzzing in the ear.

def: adj. (f. -ī), Having a bad or offensive smell, stinking, fetid.
adj. See بساندا (2).
adj. corr. of phusāhindā, q.v. adj. (f. -ī), Having a bad or offensive smell, stinking, fetid.
s.m. Whispering ( = phus-phus; phus-phusar; q.v.s.v. phus).
s.f. Chaff ( = bhūsī, q.v.); rubbish. s.f. Bran; chaff.
adj. (f. -ī) = phasphasā, q.v.
more: adj. 1. Weak, without strength. 2. Slack (as, a knot).

def: s.f. A soft or noiseless passing of wind; (fig.) whispered calumny.
s.f. = H پهسلاو फुसलाव phuslāʼo, s.m. = H پهسلاوا फुसलावा phuslāwā, s.m. (cf. phasāʼo, &c.), Coaxing, wheedling, cajoling; cajolery, flattery, seduction, blandishment.
v.t. To entice away; to kidnap.
more: v. a. 1. To coax; wheedle; cajole; flatter; humour; amuse; divert. Chūran, golī, anār-dānā, bāl-bachchoṅ kā phuslānā . Dehli street cry. Digestive powder, balls, pomegranates too, all children's hearts to woo. phuslā-ke le jānā , v. a. To kidnap; entice away.
v. a. To coax, wheedle, cajole, flatter, instigate, entice, seduce.
v. a. To fondle, to decoy.

def: s.m. = H پهسلاوا फुसलावा phuslāwā, s.m. = H پهسلاهٿ फुसलाहट phuslāhaṭ, s.f. (cf. phasāʼo, &c.), Coaxing, wheedling, cajoling; cajolery, flattery, seduction, blandishment.
s.f. contrac. of phuslāhaṭ, q.v.
more: (from پهسلانا phuslānā ) s. (dakh.) Coaxing, wheedling, blandishment ( Harris ).

ety: rt. of phuslā-nā+ū = Prk. इउओ = S. (इ)तृ+कः
def: s.m.f. Coaxing, wheedling; — coaxer, wheedler, seducer, &c.
(from پهسلانا) s. m. A coaxer, wheedler, seducer.
s.m. Coaxer, wheedler, flatterer, seducer.
more: (from پهسلانا) s. m. A wheedler, a coaxer.

def: s.m. = H پهسلاو फुसलाव phuslāʼo, s.m. = H پهسلاهٿ फुसलाहट phuslāhaṭ, s.f. (cf. phasāʼo, &c.), Coaxing, wheedling, cajoling; cajolery, flattery, seduction, blandishment.
n. f. 1. Looseness; flabbiness. 2. Whispering.

adj. (f. -ī), Soft, pulpy, spongy, loose, flabby, flaccid; slack, not rigid; breaking or separating readily and with a soft noise; weak, feeble, flimsy, softly brittle, rotten, mouldering, crumbling; worthless, bad, pithless, vapid.
adj. 1. Flabby; loose; not rigid; slack. 2. Mouldering; crumbling; weak; rotten.
s.f. Whispering; buzzing in the ear.
v.n. To whisper.
more: E. v. n. To whisper ( kānā-phūsī k .).

def: (from پهسپهسانا) s. f. Whispering, buzzing in the ear.
s.f. Whispering; buzzing in the ear.

ety: phūsa+Prk. डो = S. रः or लः
def: s.m. A rag, tatters; rubbish, litter; the shag or nap of cloth; the down of fruit; — a little brat.

ety: phūsa+Prk. डो = S. रः or लः
def: s.m. A rag, tatters; rubbish, litter; the shag or nap of cloth; the down of fruit; — a little brat.
adj. Odd, single, unmatched, unpaired, uneven; separated, detached, isolated; dispersed (as horsemen).
s.m. A foot (measure).

ety: past conj. part of phūṭnā; and = Prk. फुट्टिअ = S. स्फुटित्वा
def: adv. Out; forth; aloud, violently, bitterly (used in comp.)
subs: v.n. To start or gush forth (tears, &c.)
phūṭ-kar ronā, phūṭ-phūṭ ronā, v.n. To weep bitterly or aloud
phūṭ-phūṭ-ke nikalnā, v.n. To break out (an eruption).

def: adj. Severed, separated, &c.; odd, unpaired, &c. ( = phuṭ, q.v.)
s.f. The musk melon, Cucumis momordica; — a cucumber run to seed and bursting elastically (as the Cucumis utilissimus, Roxb., and Momordica mixta, (Roxb.).
adj. Arrogant, contemptuous, disdainful.
more: adj. H. phaṭnā to be separated. Single; odd; uneven; unmatched. phuṭ gāṅoṅ , n. m. A detached or isolated village.

intj. S. स्फिट to despise. Fie! shame! for shame! out upon you! 1. Kyā kahūṅ aur, be-hayā tujh ko, Phiṭṭe mūṅh, lānat-i-khudā tujh ko! Wom. Shauq. 2. Phiṭ jīvan us mard kā jis ghar chhoṭī joë, Ek oṛ ko paṛ rahe, khāṭ ṭaṭolan hoë . Play. Chaubolā. A shameful lot that man's is sure whose wife is all too small, Rolled up somewhere she is, he gropes and feels the bed o'er all. phiṭ phiṭ karnā , v. a. To sneer at; taunt; speak out; cry shame on. Phiṭ phiṭ kare use sab dunyā, Jo dhandā kar khāve nā . Rus. Song. The world cries shame upon the man, Who works not but eats all he can.

def: s.f. Crack, split, break, flaw; opening, gap, breach, fissure, rift; separation; cracked or broken vessels (of metal, or crockery); — difference (of opinion), misunderstanding, discord, dissension, split, quarrel, feud
more: s. f. 1. A kind of melon (Cucumis momordica: Roxb. ). 2. A ripe cucumber bursting elastically (as the Cucumis utilatissimus: Roxb. and Momordica mixta: Roxb. ). 3. ( स्फुट ) Odd, unpaired. 4. Difference of opinion, dissension, discord. 5. Separation. 6. A flaw, breach, break. پهوٿ بکهيرا phūṭ bikherā, adj. Scattered, dispersed. پهوٿ پڙنا phūṭ paṛnā, To arise (dissension). پهوٿ پهوٿ ياپهوٿ کررونا phūṭ phūṭ or phūṭ kar ronā, To weep excessively, to burst into tears. پهوٿ رهنا phūṭ rahnā, 1. To be broken. 2. To be dispersed. 3. To be unpaired. پهوت هونا phūṭ honā, To be divided in opinion.
subs: (-meṅ), Dissension to arise or take place (between or among), to disagree, be divided (in opinion)
(-meṅ), To sow discord (between or among), to set by the ears

ety: perf. part. of phūṭnā; and = Prk. फुट्टिओ = S. स्फुटित+कः
def: part. adj. (f. -ī), Broken, cracked, chipped, burst, shivered
more: s. m. (dakh.) Gorge (of a bird: Harris ).
subs: adv. With a distorted or wry face.

def: adj. Cracked; chipped; broken ( ṭūṭā ). phūṭ'an, haṛ-phūṭan , n. f. Pain in the joints or bones.
s.f. The expanded hood of a snake.
s.f. = phuṭkī, q.v. s.f. The tailor-bird, Orthotomus longicauda (cf. phudkī).
s.f. Racking pain (in the bones or joints = har-phūṭan); — misunderstanding, disagreement, discord, wrangling ( = phūṭ).
more: (see پهوٿنا) s. f. Wrangling, disagreement, misunderstanding.

def: v.t. To cause (water, &c.) to boil; — to increase; to promote, &c. (see phūṭnā).
s.f. Disagreement; disparity, &c. ( = phūṭ).
more: s. f. A sort of melon (Cucumis momordica).

def: s.f. The inusk melon ( = phūṭ, q.v.). adj. Arrogant, contemptuous, disdainful.
adj. Odd, separated, &c. = phuṭ-kar, phuṭ; — separate, incurred at different places (as debts), sundry, miscellaneous, odds and ends of.

adj. 1. Single. See phuṭ. Ajab tarah kī ye raṅgīṅ chaupaṛ, garaz bichhāī hai yāṅ Khudā ne, Koī hai phuṭkal, kisī kā jug hai, phireṅ haiṅ nardeṅ ye khāne khāne, Jo pāsā pheṅkeṅ banā-banā-kar, aur dāoṅ kitne hī dil meṅ ṭhāṅeṅ, Jo chāhtā hai aṭhārah āveṅ, to uske paṛte haiṅ tīn kāne . (The world compared to a chessboard). Nazīr. A wondrous chequered board diverse this earth by God laid out, Some odd, some even, wandering are from square to square about, Some throw the dice with artful dodge by many thought afore, Who wish eighteen to throw get but three odd ones for their score. 2. Sundry; miscellaneous. Phuṭkal hisāb āj tai kar lo .
adj. See s.v. phut. adj. Arrogant, contemptuous, disdainful.
more: n. f. A curse; malediction. phiṭkār barasnā , v. n. To be accursed; to be doomed to wander on the face of the earth.
n. m. 1. The sound of a lash in the air, in winnowing, or in beating clothes against a stone, etc. 2. A long whip used in training horses.

def: adj. Odd, &c. = phuṭ, q.v.; — arrogant, over-bearing. adj. Arrogant, contemptuous, disdainful.
more: adj. 1. Odd, unpaired. 2. Separate, dispersed (as, a horseman).

def: adv. = phūṭ, q.v. adj. Arrogant, contemptuous, disdainful.
adv. = phūṭ, q.v. adj. Arrogant, contemptuous, disdainful.
s.f. The tailor-bird, Orthotomus longicauda (cf. phudkī).
more: n. f. The Indian tailor-bird; orthotomus longicauda . Its cry is to-wee-to-wee-to-wee .
n. f. 1. A blot; spot; stain; splash. 2. The undissolved portions of any soluble matter.
n. f. 1. A fowler's net. 2. A large cage. 3. A long rope tied to a rattle to frighten birds.

def: s.f. A blot, spot, stain, speck; splash; — the undissolved portions of any soluble matter.
more: s. f. A blot, spot, stain. ¶ phaṭkī, s. f. 1. A fowlerʼs net. 2. A large cage. 3. A rope tied to a tree to frighten birds with its sound.

def: adj. Odd, &c. = phuṭ, q.v. adj. Arrogant, contemptuous, disdainful.
adj. Cracked, bad, base (coin).
v.n. = phūṭnā, q.v.
more: v. n. (dakh. i. q. ترقي پانا) To get promotion or advancement (?).
v. n. (dakh.) To rise: see پهوٿنا phūṭnā.

ety: phūṭ˚ = Prk. फुट्ट(इ) = S. स्फुट्य(ते), passive of rt. स्फुट्;+nā = anā = Prk.अणअं = S. अनीयं
def: v.n. To be broken, to be broken into; to be broken down; to be dispersed, be separated, be detached; to separate; to be unpaired; to break, crack, split, burst; to break out or forth; to sprout, shoot, bud, germinate; to burst out or forth, to gush out, to escape (from, -se); to burst out crying; to come into view, to appear, to rise, be spread, be diffused (as a smell, &c.); to be made public, to transpire, to leak out, get wind; to boil (as water); to flourish, get preferment or promotion
more: v. n. 1. To be broken, to be broken into or broken down. 2. To be dispersed, to be separated, to separate, to be unpaired. 3. To burst, split. 4. To be made publick, to transpire. 5. To arise (as, a smell) or to burst forth. 6. To get promotion or advancement.
subs: v.n. (intensive), To break to pieces, to burst asunder, &c.
v.n. To be broken; to be dispersed; to be unpaired.

def: v. n. 1. To crack; split; break; to be rent or broken. 1. Aisī laṛāī huī ki bīsyoṅ sir phūṭ gaë 2. Kitnoṅ ne apne gam se ab hai yeh gat banāī, Maile kuchaile kapṛe, āṅkheṅ bhī ḍabḍabāī, Nā ghar meṅ jhūlā ḍālā, na oṛhnī raṅgāī, Phūṭā paṛā hai chūlhā, ṭūṭī paṛī kaṛāhī. Birāh . The pain of absence. Nazīr. The state of numbers sunk, alas! to that degree, All mean and filthy clad, the tear drops in their ee, Nor swinging in the yard, nor garment of gay dye, The cooking place, the frying-pan, all broken lie. 2. To break out or forth; to have an eruption. Tamām badan phūṭ niklā . 3. To sprout; shoot; germinate. Koṅpal phūṭnā . 4. To spring; burst; gush out; erupt. Pānī phūṭnā . 5. To boil (water). 6. To rise; appear; come into view. 7. To get wind; to be diffused (a smell or report); to be made public. Santan prati baiṭh baiṭh lok-lāj khoī, Ab to bāt phūṭ gaī jānat sab koī, Mere man Rām nām, dūsrā na koī . Mīrāṅ Bāi. (A poetess.) With holy men I've often sat, the world's scorn shown, The secret now is out, by every body known— No other name dwells in my heart save Ram alone. 8. To break silence; utter; speak. Muṅh se itnā bhī na phūṭā 'āiyë' . Raṅgīṅ. 9. To lose an eye; to be blind. Phūṭī saheṅ, āṅjhī na saheṅ . Prov. He would rather lose his eye than spend a farthing on anjan (to save it). 10. To be detached, separated; to deviate, turn aside. phūṭe dīdoṅ, phūṭī nazroṅ , Wom. adv. Not at all. Chhīṅkā, rassī, kanjriyoṅ kā sā yeh laṭkāūṅ maiṅ, nauj! Phūṭī nazroṅ, Chāṅd Khāṅ, bhātā nahīṅ jhūmar mujhe . Wom. Rāhat. phūṭe muṅh se , adv. With a wry face. Ek bosah ham ne māṅgā, rāh-i-maulā, wāh jī! Phūṭe muṅh se yeh na niklā, 'lete jāo, Shāh jī .' I asked her for a kiss—'in God's name grant as charity,' Zounds Sir! not even crossly would she say 'take it, Shah Ji.'
adv. = phūṭ-phūṭ, q.v.s.v. phūṭnā.
adj. Odd, unpaired, &c. = phuṭ, q.v. adj. Arrogant, contemptuous, disdainful.
s.f. Small fine rain, drizzle; mist, fog; small spot or drop
more: n. f. Small drops of rain; drizzle; Scotch mist. phuār-dār bābarlaiṭ , n. f. Small spotted bobbinet. phuār paṛnā , v. n. To fall in small drops; to drizzle.

n. f. 1. A sloven; slattern; slipshod. 1. Phūaṛ chāle, nau ghar hāle . Prov. When slipshods walk nine houses shake. 2. Aë Chail suhāvan, phūaṛ mail chhuṛāvan . Prov. Now blooming April's come, washed out the slattern's scum (with perspiration). 2. A fool; a stupid; ninny; a silly. 1. Badlī meṅ din na dīse, phūaṛ baiṭhī pīse . Prov. For the clouds, she cannot see it's day, and so the booby grinds and grinds all day. 2. Phūaṛ kā māl haṅs haṅs khāo . Prov. A fool's the owner, ha-ha-ha! we will enjoy it for him, ha-ha-ha! 3. ( phūṛā ). One given to talk obscenely. phūaṛ-pan , n. m. Slovenliness; untidiness; awkwardness.
subs: v.n. Small drops of rain to fall; to drizzle
adj. Having small spots, marked with small spots, spotted.

def: adj. & s.f. = phūhaṛ, q.v.
s.m. A fountain, a jet-dʼeau ( = fawārā); a watering-can with a rose; the perpendicular tube of a ḥuqqa.
more: n. m. 1. A fountain; a jet d'eau . 2. A watering can with a rose. 3. See āb-i-naë .

def: s. (dakh.) Drizzling, misling rain ( Harris ).
s.f. Fat, &c. = pīh, q.v. s.f. Fat, grease, tallow.
initialism of پْریس ٹْرَسْٹ آف اِنْڈِیا (pres ṭrasṭ āf inḍiyā); initialism of پاکِسْتان تَحْرِیکِ اِنْصَاف (pākistān tahrīk-i insāf)
adj. & s.m. = piyā, q.v. adj. & s.m. Beloved, dear; — dear one, husband; sweetheart, lover ( = pī; piʼū).
more: 1. adj. Beloved. 2. s. m. A lover, a sweetheart, a husband.

def: s.f. Pus, matter, purulent discharge (from a sore, &c.)
more: s. f. Pus, matter, corruption, purulent running.

def: s.f. Suppuration, mattering.
s.f. = H پيبيانا पीबियाना pībiyānā, v.n. See pibiyānā and pibiyāhaṭ the correct forms).
v.n. To become mattery, to suppurate (a sore, &c.).
v.n. = H پيبياهٿ पीबियाहट pībiyāhaṭ, s.f. See pibiyānā and pibiyāhaṭ the correct forms).
s.f. Water in which rice has been boiled, rice-gruel; gruel ( = pīćh).
more: n. f. S. पिच्छ rice gruel. Rice-water; water in which rice has been boiled.

n. m. P. پيچيدن to twist. 1. Entanglement. See uljhāo (1—3). Pagyā bāṅdhe, pech saṅvāre, tel lagāve julphan meṅ; Tere dayā dharam nahīṅ man meṅ; Mukhṛā kyā dekhe darpan meṅ? Kabīr. Your turban you bind, its folds you adjust, and you oil and you smoothen your hair; Nor virtue nor love in your heart, your face to admire in your glass all your care. 2. A roll of cloth, etc. 3. Windings. See pher . 4. Tactics; stratagem ( chāl ). 5. Equivoque; ambiguity; duplicity. 6. Any trick in wrestling. 7. A screw; ( Pop .) a machine. pech paṛnā , v. n. 1. To become complicated, difficult or intricate. 2. To be entangled. pech denā , v. n. To circumvent; twist; screw; deceive. pech kā chhallā , A thread of a screw. pech khāë huë , ( Bot .) Convolute ( bal-dār ).

(from پيچيدن) s. m. f. 1. Twist, revolution, a coil, a plait, fold. 2. A screw. 3. Perplexity, ambiguity, maze, difficulty, trouble. 4. Deceit. 5. Complication, involution. پيچ اٿهانا pech uṭhānā, To suffer loss or distress. پيچ اکهڙنا pech ukhaṛnā, v. n. To loosen (the twist of any thing). پيچ باندهنا pech bāndhnā, To grapple in wrestling. پيچ پاچ pech-pāch or پيچ پانچ pech-pānch, s. m. Twist, coil, &c. پيچ پڙنا pech paṛnạ̄, To become difficult or intricate, to occur as an obstacle, to be entangled. پيچ چلنا pech chalna, To prevail by stratagem. پيچ چنا pech chunnā, To bind the twists of a turband with care. پيچ دار pech-dār, adj. Twisted, coiled, spiral, crooked, intricate. پيچ درپيچ pech-dar-pech, Coil within coil, entangled, complex, intricate. پيچ دينا pech denā, To circumvent, to deceive, to twist, to screw, &c. پيچ ڐالنا pech ḍālnā, 1. To throw obstacles in the way. 2. (in playing…

def: s.m. A wrestler.
more: n. f. A wrestler. pichaitī , n. f. The art of wrestling.

def: s.f. Wrestling.

ety: pićak = Prk. पिच्चक्के(इ), or पिच्चक्क(इ), fr. S. पिच्च+कृ
def: v.n. To be pressed or squeezed, to be flattened; to be dinted; to be shrivelled; to be reduced (a swelling).
more: v. n. To be dinted, shrivelled, reduced ( paṭaknā ). Do hī din kī mehnat meṅ gāl pichak gaë .
v. n. 1. To be squeezed. 2. To be shrivelled.

def: s.m. The belly or abdomen, the stomach; the back (of an animal).
s.f. The instep; the calf of the leg.
adj. Big-bellied, corpulent.
s.m. = pać-pać, q.v. s.m.f. (?), The noise made in walking in mire, &c.; plash, splash, squash.
s.m. Inflammation of the eyes, opthalmia.

more: n. m. S. पश्चात्, , Pālī pachchā . 1. The hinder part; the rear. Pīchhā pīchhā hī hai . Prov. The last the last remains. (No time like the present). 2. Following; pursuit; prosecution; persecution. pīchhā chhuṛā'nā , v. a. To rid oneself of; shake off; get rid of. Baṛī mushkil meṅ apnā pīchhā chhuṛāyā hai . 2. To release; liberate; remove any obstacle. Khudā ke wāste merā pīchhā chhuṛā do! pīchhā bhārī honā , v. n. To have the enemy in the rear. pīchhā chhoṛnā , v. n. To give up or over; drop; cease; let alone. pīchhā karnā, yā lenā , v. a. 1. To follow; go after; pursue; chase. 2. To press; importune; prosecute. Bhāgte kā pīchhā nahīṅ karte . 3. To recoil (a gun, etc.). pīchhā na chhoṛnā , v. n. To press eagerly; hang upon; pursue to the last; persecute.

Sāṅjh sunī piyā āvan piyārī, sundar nāri siṅgār banāī, Pīyar kesar, pīyar besar, pīyar hār līye larkāī, Pīyar chīr dīyo Kamlāpatī, pīyar chandan deh lagāī, Pīyar pān kī bīrī lagī, piyārī pīrī bhaī, pīū nahīṅ āī . Kabīt. Her husband would return at eve, the loved one heard, her toilette made, With paint, nose-ring, and garland round her neck, in golden hues arrayed, Golden her vesture too, and golden sandal on her body shone, Ripe yellow pān she chewed—her spouse not come, the maid was yellow grown. pīlā-pan, pilāī , n. f. Yellowness ( zardī ). pīle-bādal , W. n. m. Break of day; dawn. pīlī , n. f. A gold-mohur ( asharfī ).
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀧𑀺𑀢𑁆𑀢𑀮 (pittala), from Sanskrit पीतल (pītala, “brass, yellow”).

ety: by met., for lipaṭnā, or ćipaṭnā
def: v.n. To adhere, to stick (to), cling (to). — pilać-ke, adv. Firmly, resolutely; with all one's might.
more: v. n. To cling; adhere to; stick to. pilach-ke , adv. With all one's might, one's best.

def: s.f. = palaʼī, q.v. s.f. Young branch or spray of a tree.
adj. & s.f. Giving to drink; one who gives to drink; price paid for drinking (used chiefly in comp., e.g. dūdh-pilāʼī dāʼī, 'A wet-nurse'; — pānī-pilāʼī, 'Price paid for drinking water').

s.f. Yellowness ( = pīlāʼī, q.v.). adj. & s.f. Giving to drink; one who gives to drink; price paid for drinking (used chiefly in comp., e.g. dūdh-pilāʼī dāʼī, 'A wet-nurse'; — pānī-pilāʼī, 'Price paid for drinking water').
s.f. The spleen; enlargement or inflammation of the spleen.
more: s. f. The spleen; a disease from enlargement or inflammation of the spleen.

def: s. f. Yellowness.
s.f. Yellowness ( = pīlā-pan).
Gaṛh, n. f. S. प्तोहा Spleen ( tillī ); enlargement of the spleen.
s.m. The large black ant.
more: n. S. पिपीलिका ant. 1. The large black ant. Tinkā girā gaiṅd mukh, nek na ghaṭeo ahār, So le chalī papīlikā pālan ko pariwār . Dohā. A grain fell from the elephant's food, he missed it not a jot, An ant conveyed it to his nest a bellyful his family got. 2. The golden oriole, known as the mango bird. It builds in May a cup-shaped nest hanging in the fork of a branch, lays white eggs with a few black spots at the larger end, and has in the breeding season a whistle something like a koel's (cuckoo).

def: s.m. The Indian oriole (vulgarly called the Mango-bird); the small yellow-headed bird Loxia philippensis ( = pīt-mastak, q.v.s.v. pīta).
s.m. Satin.

v.t. To cause to drink, to give a drink of water, &c. to; to take (an animal) to water; to water, irrigate; to cause to take in or absorb; to fill, charge, prime; to cause to swallow, to thrust down one's throat, make one believe; to give a blow, send through (as a ball, &c.)
more: v. a. S. पा to drink. 1. To cause to drink; take to the water; to water; give a drink of water. 2. To give milk to one's young; to suckle; nurse. 3. To cause to absorb or take in. 4. To charge; fill; prime. 5. To cause to go down with one; make believe. 6. To give a blow; shoot; send (a ball, etc. through one). pānī pilā'ī , n. f. The charge for a drink of water.
(caus. of پينا) v. a. To give or cause to drink.
subs: s.m. One who gives to drink, a cup-bearer

ety: pīlā, q.v.+pan = Prk. प्पणं = त्वणं = S. त्वं (Vedic त्वनं
def: s.m. Yellowness.
s.f. A drink, a draught.
more: Hin. n. f. A draught; a drink.
